citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
In discussions with the AV, she was doing rounds on Tuesday Feb 17th and had informed the staff that a single penguin
juvenile could not be housed in the kitchen prep area and should be moved back to penguin building. She also stated
another two penguin chicks that were housed in a bin should be moved to the penguin holding area for husbandry
purposes. During the inspection process on Wednesday Feb 18th, the juvenile penguin was housed in an office space,
and the other two penguin chicks were housed in a black tub in a dark office at the encounter building. The facility did not
follow guidance provided by the AV which negates the veterinary authority over the care and use of animals at the park.
Exhibitors shall assure that the attending veterinarian has appropriate authority to ensure the provision of adequate
veterinary care and to oversee the adequacy of other aspects of animal care and use.
Correct Feb 20, 2026

The facility has a walk-through aviary with approximately 100 budgies, where guests are allowed to roam throughout the
aviary up to perching and nest boxes and feed budgies off a stick. When the park was open for the season, from
approximately July 26- Oct 10, 2025, at least 10 budgies were stepped on by a guest or died of unidentified trauma.
Frequently budgies will land on the ground and failure to maintain sufficient distance or barriers between the guests and
the budgies led to being stepped on.
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with
sufficient distance and or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of the
animals and the public.
Correct by: April 15, 2026

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
Cleaning is not sufficient in the Baboon, squirrel monkey, and ring-tailed lemur enclosures. There is an accumulation of
feces in and on the hide boxes, and along with the fire hoses used for climbing. There is excess debris trapped between
the window and wire mesh in the baboon enclosure. These non human primate enclosures must be spot cleaned daily
and to prevent this accumulation and to prevent the animals from becoming soiled. Methods and frequency of cleaning
need to be addressed for the health of the animals.
Correct by: Feb 23, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(a)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
In discussions with the AV, she was doing rounds on Tuesday Feb 17th and had informed the staff that a single penguin 
juvenile could not be housed in the kitchen prep area and should be moved back to penguin building. She also stated 
another two penguin chicks that were housed in a bin should be moved to the penguin holding area for husbandry 
purposes. During the inspection process on Wednesday Feb 18th, the juvenile penguin was housed in an office space, 
and the other two penguin chicks were housed in a black tub in a dark office at the encounter building. The facility did not 
follow guidance provided by the AV which negates the veterinary authority over the care and use of animals at the park.  
 
Exhibitors shall assure that the attending veterinarian has appropriate authority to ensure the provision of adequate 
veterinary care and to oversee the adequacy of other aspects of animal care and use. 
 
Correct Feb 20, 2026 
2.131(c)(1)  Critical
   
Handling of animals. 
The facility has a walk-through aviary with approximately 100 budgies, where guests are allowed to roam throughout the 
aviary up to perching and nest boxes and feed budgies off a stick. When the park was open for the season, from 
approximately July 26- Oct 10, 2025, at least 10 budgies were stepped on by a guest or died of unidentified trauma. 
Frequently budgies will land on the ground and failure to maintain sufficient distance or barriers between the guests and 
the budgies led to being stepped on.  
 
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with 
sufficient distance and or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of the 
animals and the public. 
 
Correct by: April 15, 2026 
3.84(a) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.

Cleaning is not sufficient in the Baboon, squirrel monkey, and ring-tailed lemur enclosures. There is an accumulation of 
feces in and on the hide boxes, and along with the fire hoses used for climbing. There is excess debris trapped between 
the window and wire mesh in the baboon enclosure. These non human primate enclosures must be spot cleaned daily 
and to prevent this accumulation and to prevent the animals from becoming soiled. Methods and frequency of cleaning 
need to be addressed for the health of the animals.  
Correct by: Feb 23, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
